2019 Annual Report
Stock code 1299
Purpose leads the way forward

AIA Holdings Limited and its Affiliates (collectively, "AIA" or "The Group") is the largest pan-Asian Independently listed life insurance group covering 18 markets in Asia Pacific, including Hong Kong Special Administrative Region, Thailand, Singapore, Malaysia, Mainland China, South Korea, Philippines, Australia, Indonesia, Taiwan, China, Special trip to Vietnam, New Zealand and Macau Administrative Region, Brunei Darussalam, Cambodia and Myanmar There are wholly-owned branches and subsidiaries, 99% interest in Sri Lanka subsidiary and a 49% interest in the Indian joint venture.

AIA's business achievements today can be Dating back to 1919 a century ago in The birthplace of Shanghai. According to life insurance premium Computing, the Group operates in Asia Pacific (Japan (except) leading the industry and operating in most markets The field firmly occupies a leadership position. As of 2019 As of December 31, the group's total asset value was $284 billion.

AIA offers a range of products and Services covering life insurance, accident and medical Insurance and savings plans to suit individuals Customers' long-term savings and protection Needed. In addition, the Group is also an enterprise Client provides employee benefits, credit insurance and retirement security services. The group through A large number of exclusive agents throughout the Asia-Pacific region, A network of partners and employees for more than 36 million individual policy holders and more than 16 million group insurance plans Provide services to participating members.

AIA Holdings Limited in Hong Kong Listed on the Main Board of The Stock Exchange Limited (Stock code: "1299"); Qimei National Depositary Receipts (Level 1) off-market Buy and sell on the easy market (the transaction number is "AAGIY").

our vision is to become a global leader Life Insurance Provider.

P.S.: (1) Financial information from 2017 to 2019 is based on the fiscal year ending on December 31 Presentation. Financial information for 2015 to 2016 is based on the fiscal year ended November 30 Baseline presentation. For clarity, growth rates are based on fiscal year ending December 31 Baseline presentation.

(2) The value of new business is the estimated statutory profit after tax generated in the future from the new business sold during the period The present value calculated at the time of sale is deducted from any excess statutory reserves held to support new business. The cost of the funds required.

(3) Annualized new premium is an indicator to measure the level of new business, calculated as annualized before reinsurance ceded Calculated as the sum of 100% of the first-year premium and 10% of the single premium.
(4) Operating profit after tax is presented net of non-controlling interests.
(5) Total weighted premium income includes 100% of renewal premiums before reinsurance cession, first-year premiums 100%, and 10% of the single premium.

(6) Intrinsic value is determined using actuarial methods based on a series of specific assumptions about future experience. The estimated economic value of life insurance business, but does not include the economic value of any future new business. Intrinsic value equity is the sum of intrinsic value, goodwill and other intangible assets.

(7) The new business value of each segment includes pension business, which is presented based on local legal basis and is not included in Group headquarters expenses are not allocated and non-controlling interests are not deducted.

(8) In 2019, annualized new premiums and new business value in other markets included our joint venture Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited (Tata AIA Life) 49% of performance to reflect our equity holdings. Annualized new premiums reported in 2018 and new business value are not restated and excludes any Contribution. The overall new business value reported by the Group in 2019 does not include non-controlling The value of new business attributable to equity. The value of new business in 2018 has not been restated, as previously reported As disclosed in the 2018 annual report, this figure was reported without deducting non-controlling interests. Tata AIA Life's IFRS results are calculated using the equity method of accounting. of the Group The consolidated financial statements use Tata AIA Life's financial information on a lagged one quarter basis. For clarity, total weighted premium income excludes any contribution from Tata AIA Life.

| AIA Insurance Holdings Limited 006
Overview
Chairman's report
During the year, consumer confidence was affected by different geopolitical events, including uncertainty over Sino-US trade negotiations and social unrest in Hong Kong. in this background
Next, AIA continued to record growth across all major financial indicators.
Despite the headwinds from the external environment, especially the events in Hong Kong, the value of new business increased to US$4.154 billion; excluding business in Hong Kong, the Group's
New business value rose 16%. Operating profit after tax increased by 9% to US$5.741 billion, while underlying free surplus increased by 13% to US$5.501 billion.
Growth rates are presented at constant exchange rates to provide a clearer picture of the performance of the underlying business on a year-on-year basis.
On an embedded value basis, equity attributable to shareholders of AIA Holdings Limited (the Company) (embedded value equity) increased by 12% during the year to US$63.905 billion.
As of December 31, 2019, our principal regulated operating company AIA Co.
The solvency adequacy ratio of the Group was 362%, and the Group's free surplus in excess of the required regulatory capital reached US$14.917 billion, reflecting the Group's financial
Strength.

The Board of Directors (Board of Directors) composed of directors of the Company recommends the payment of a final dividend of HK93.30 cents per share, adhering to AIA's long-established principles of prudence, sustainability and
The progressive dividend payment policy allows the Group to seize future growth opportunities and maintain financial flexibility under the current macroeconomic and capital market environment.
The long-term development opportunities for AIA in the Asia-Pacific region are huge: as the number of people in the region with middle-class incomes and lifestyles increases, their appetite for
The need for savings and financial security will continue to rise. At the same time, under-insurance remains significant across Asia, and governments in many markets
Only limited social welfare protection is provided. This "protection gap" provides important opportunities for AIA, the leading life insurance group in the pan-Asian region.
Meet customer needs.
2019 marks AIA's 100th anniversary, and everyone in our group is proud of it. Since the story of AIA began in Shanghai in 1919, to the latest
Our business in Myanmar was put into operation in November 2019, and we held various activities in the 18 markets covered to celebrate this significant milestone.
Our employees and their families joined in the celebrations, along with AIA's customers, friends and partners, including representatives from Tottenham Hotspur Football Club (Tottenham Hotspur)
Our extraordinary journey. On behalf of the Board of Directors, I would like to express my sincere gratitude to all AIA's customers, employees, agents and partners for their many years of
Since then, our dedicated efforts and unremitting efforts have created the outstanding achievements of the Group today. AIA's 100th anniversary gives us an opportunity to thank the past and present
People who have made great contributions to the Group and move forward with confidence into the next century.
AIA has a long history in Asia and has experienced many different market cycles.
A stable and reliable brand has been established. Despite the challenging operating environment, AIA
With the strength and tenacity of our business and the scale of AIA's opportunities in Asia Pacific,
Continue to create growth in 2019.
2019 Annual Report | 007
Overview Financial and Operational Review corporate governance Other information financial statements
Mr. Xie Shirong Independent non-executive chairman
AIA understands that climate change poses serious, large-scale and complex challenges, so we are committed to doing our part and transitioning to a more sustainable future.
Our efforts to address and enhance relevant disclosures on critical environmental, social and governance issues have earned us international recognition.
In 2019, AIA was selected by rating agency Sustainalytics to be among the top one percent of companies with the best performance in environmental, social and governance risk management.
Ranked second among 253 companies. In addition, we have been included in the FTSE4Good Index Series for the third consecutive year.
Since 2018, AIA has joined the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures.
The global organization works to establish a voluntary and consistent disclosure framework on the impacts of climate change. In 2019, we used this to match the
1,400 companies, and more than 30 external investment managers were contacted to learn more about how these companies manage climate change risks.
Our Group Chief Executive Officer and President, Mr. Ng Keng Hooi, will retire on May 31, 2020. On behalf of all members of the Board of Directors,
My heartfelt thanks to Mr. Huang for his service to AIA since the listing of the Group, during his tenure as Group CEO and President and previously as Regional CEO.
Made significant and far-reaching contributions.
| AIA Insurance Holdings Limited 008
Overview
Chairman's report

I also welcome Mr. Li Yuanxiang, the Group Chief Executive Officer and President-designate, to join the Group. Mr. Li has an outstanding leadership track record in the industry and possesses excellent
Conditions to continue and expand on Mr. Huang Jinghui's outstanding work as the Group's CEO and President. Mr. Li Yuanxiang's appointment was carefully reviewed by AIA
Successor selection process to appoint the best candidate to take over Mr. Huang's work. We expect that under Mr. Li's leadership and contributions, AIA will continue to
Living up to its brand promise in Asia.
As the Group enters the transition period of executive leadership, the Board will continue to uphold the highest standards of corporate governance. All non-executive directors on the board
They are all independent individuals who bring extensive governance and executive experience to the work of the Board. We adopt effective governance policies and cooperate with AIA
Efforts to promote corporate culture enable us to effectively manage risk in an increasingly complex environment. In addition to focusing on sustainable development, we also continue to
Coordinate closely with senior management on data privacy, cybersecurity and ethical business practices.
In the first quarter of 2020, the world faced the uncertain impact of COVID-19 and the measures to control its spread. most important to the Group's financial performance

The ultimate impact will depend on the duration and severity of the outbreak, and we are closely monitoring the situation. Restrictions on overseas travel coupled with reduced face-to-face communication,
It has had a significant impact on the Group's new business sales in the first quarter. We always value the health and well-being of our employees and agents. Affected by the epidemic
region, our business teams are also committed to supporting the communities we serve during this difficult time.
I am confident that AIA is well-positioned to continue delivering long-term growth by serving our communities in Asia. We are in the promotion zone

The goal of assuming a leadership role in the process of economic and social development has also become more important. Thank you for supporting us in realizing our vision and helping customers live a "healthy" life.
A long and good life."
Xie Shirong
Independent non-executive chairman
March 12, 2020

Report from the Group CEO and President

AIA's solid performance in 2019 is a testament to our diversified and high-quality business portfolio, as well as the market's strong demand for protection and long-term insurance in the markets in which we operate.
There is growing structural demand for savings products.

The Group's overall new business value increased by 6%, reflecting very strong growth in the first half of the year. However, the performance in the second half of the year was affected by the social environment in Hong Kong.
Unrest has a significant impact. Excluding Hong Kong operations, the Group's overall new business value for the year increased by 16%. Operating profit after tax increased by 9%, resulting from basic
Free earnings increased by 13%, while equity attributable to shareholders of AIA Holdings Limited (the Company) on an embedded value basis (embedded value equity) also increased strongly
Strong, to $63.9 billion, up 12% after excluding dividends. As in previous years, growth rates are presented at constant exchange rates to provide a clearer picture of the underlying industries.
performance on a year-to-year basis.
Despite the challenging operating environment, AIA's key financial indicators improved
Continue to record growth.

and progressive dividend policy, allowing the Group to seize future growth opportunities and maintain financial flexibility under the current macroeconomic and capital market environment.
sex. The full-year dividend is HK126.60 cents per share, an increase of 11% from the full-year dividend in 2018 (excluding special dividends).
In 2019, some of the markets in which we operate faced many challenges, including declining consumer confidence and rising political and trade tensions. especially

It is the decrease in the number of tourists from mainland China in Hong Kong in the second half of the year that will have an impact on new business sales in our largest market. Despite the short-term impact on business, AIA
The long-term growth opportunities for insurance remain substantial and we will continue to benefit from the increasingly prosperous Asian markets, private insurance penetration and social
Benefit coverage will be low.
2019 marks AIA's 100th anniversary. The Group expresses its gratitude to all friends who have made dedicated efforts to promote economic and social progress in Asia over the past century.
Bang Insurance employees. Over the past century, AIA has successfully navigated dramatic economic and political changes to become one of the world's largest life insurance companies.
We are deeply proud of AIA's contribution to the development of local financial markets and the coverage and effectiveness of life insurance in the region. We are also grateful to the district
Millions of customers and their families build long-lasting relationships. In many cases, these trusting relationships span multiple generations.
AIA has its roots in Shanghai and China remains at the heart of our growth strategy. The Chinese government announced further measures to lift restrictions on foreign ownership and
Continuing to open up the life insurance industry will create a historic opportunity for AIA. We have submitted regulatory approval applications for converting our Shanghai branch into a subsidiary.
Please. Subject to regulatory approval, the newly established subsidiary will lay a new foundation for us to expand our business to other regions in mainland China.
The life insurance industry has the responsibility to promote the well-being of the community, provide support to them in times of economic hardship and critical illness, and use effective methods to
Pool savings to help them secure income in retirement. However, the Asian market's protection in terms of life protection, medical insurance, pensions and even retirement savings
Still woefully inadequate. These growing demands motivate us to continue to move forward, while also highlighting the huge growth potential of the markets in which we operate.
When it comes to well-being, I am particularly proud of AIA's industry leadership, which reflects our commitment to delivering on the AIA brand promise and helping the world

Let's all live a healthy, long-lasting and good life. We continue to transform from the traditional transaction-based insurance business model and are committed to becoming a lifelong partner of our customers and helping them
They live healthier lifestyles, including through our comprehensive, scientifically data-based wellness program, AIA Vitality. This transformation is changing me
We have more and more contacts with customers in the region. I believe that inspiring people to improve health and wellness benefits AIA, our customers and our communities
Bringing a win-win situation.
With AIA's trusted distribution strength, market-leading brands and long history in Asia, we can provide customers with assurance and protect their future.
Come health and financial security. AIA is the leading life insurance company in the pan-Asia region and we will continue to build on this position, led by a dedicated team
Remain focused on executing our clear growth strategy and maintaining our competitive advantage.

offset by a significant decline in customer base. Throughout the year, our agency business recorded double-digit growth in new business value from local customers in Hong Kong, as we focused on "the most

significant contribution to growth. The strong momentum in these markets reflects our ability to quickly launch new partnerships by drawing on the experience gained from the Group's other regional operations.
partnerships and achieve better results.
Branding and Marketing
Leveraging on AIA's long history in the Pan-Asian region, AIA has now developed into the largest pan-Asian life insurance company with Asia's leading insurance products.
brand, trusted by millions of customers. Our goal leads our commitment - to bring positive results by helping people live a healthy, long-term and good life.
Influence is particularly true in today's society, and it is also the core concept of our customer service. We continue to work with a range of partners to promote healthy living,
This includes AIA Global Ambassador David Beckham and key global partnerships with Tottenham Hotspur Football Club (Tottenham Hotspur).
To help customers improve their health and well-being, AIA has established a unique strategic framework in risk assessment, prevention, diagnosis, and treatment.
We provide support to customers throughout their health journey. "AIA Vitality" and our wellness programs encourage clients to practice good lifestyle habits to cope with
Regarding the rapid increase in lifestyle-related diseases in Asia. We currently offer comprehensive health plans in 12 markets. As of the end of December 2019,
The Group's total health plan membership exceeded 1.7 million, an increase of 42% from the end of 2018.
Our brand promise, partnerships and wellness programs are the strengths that AIA employees and distribution are most proud of. Not only through various education and

The outreach program promotes a variety of health and wellness issues in the region, while also improving the effectiveness of treatments and providing urgently needed services by significantly improving the health of clients.
financial protection and create value for customers.
2019 Annual Report | 013
Overview Financial and Operational Review corporate governance Other information financial statements
Technology and operations

Our approach to investing in and leveraging new technologies is to deliver significant value and enhance capabilities, thereby increasing efficiencies and improving service to customers, employees and distribution.
services, improve productivity and optimize experience.
In 2019, we significantly enhanced our ability to support customers in conducting transactions digitally and automated many of the back-office business processes associated with these services.
ation. Across all the markets in which we operate, more than 80% of customer interactions are now conducted digitally. In addition, on all new policies
Of these, more than 90% were submitted digitally, and automatic underwriting accounted for 62%.
We continue to optimize our proprietary agent tools and platforms to increase productivity and provide easy and direct support for key activities, including sales activity management, service
services and recruitment. Our digital recruitment platform provides content and functionality tailored to each market's unique recruiting programs and client initiatives.
In addition, we began the digital transformation of strategic bancassurance partners, using data analysis to further enhance the end-to-end customer experience and optimize the search for new
Sales targets and increase sales conversion rates. We are implementing measures across the Group to rapidly expand and further optimize these tools.

As the technology we use increases day by day, the Group continues to attach great importance to cyber security. We continue to make significant investments in cybersecurity measures, including establishing internal

In-depth forensic investigation capabilities, as well as the hiring of independent external consultants, ensure the Group's data security and test and verify the Group's measures to prevent cyber threats.
degree of stability.
People-oriented
Attracting and retaining top talent and empowering future leaders remain key to AIA's continued success. AIA upholds our philosophy

The principle of luck: "Use the right people, use the right method, do the right things...results will follow." We have cultivated the talent for more than 23,000 employees and tens of thousands of agents.
A learning-focused, collaborative culture.
In 2019, we launched customized leadership development courses to help leaders at all levels of the Group develop their talents. AIA Leadership Center
(ALC) continues to provide best-in-class talent development programs to our senior leaders, top agency executives and key partner executives. ALC

It has entered its fourth year since its opening; in 2019, ALC held more than 250 events. ALC was awarded the prestigious European Management Development Foundation (European Foundation for Management Development)
Foundation for Management Development) to recognize the outstanding performance of the AIA Leadership Development Program. Currently, AIA
Insurance is the only life insurance company in the world to receive this certification.
AIA's proactive reflection of its brand promise and an inclusive work environment that engages employees are core elements of our talent recruitment and retention strategy. I like it

However, according to the report, our performance in Gallup Q12 (Gallup Q12) annual employee engagement indicator survey has enabled the Group to rank among the top 10 in Gallup Global Finance for the third consecutive year.

Top quartile of services and insurance industry benchmarks. In addition, in 2019, the number of eligible employees participating in the long-term employee stock purchase plan reached a new high, which is a testament to the
Loyalty to AIA.
In addition, AIA was listed in Forbes "Global 2000-The World's Best Employers 2019"
list), and the company was selected as a component company of the "Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index" for the third consecutive year.

Report from the Group CEO and President
prospects
In 2019, Asian economies continued to record solid growth, with the exception of Hong Kong, which was affected by social unrest in the second half of the year. Entering the first year of 2020
